title: heterotheca rutteri
intro: Heterotheca rutteri, also known as Rutter's false goldeneye or Rutter's heterotheca, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, and has been shown to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Heterotheca rutteri can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its associated health benefits.
